evidence.scientific_clarity: Six-cluster ridgeline KDE plot clearly shows progressively higher/broader distributions from Clusters 1–2 to 3–6; x-axis labeled, ridges labeled on left as required by brief.
evidence.typography: PDF uses Type 3 bitmap font (DejaVuSans embedded as Type 3, not Type 42); no pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set in script; SVG text is rendered as paths, not selectable text.
evidence.palette: Sequential blue-to-red palette is colorblind-unfriendly (blue/red problematic for deuteranopia); however it is appropriate for an ordered/directional axis and no categorical misuse of directional colors.
evidence.layout_density: Seven-by-five-inch canvas is well used; ridges fill the panel with mild overlap (overlap=0.6); cluster labels on left are clear and non-overlapping; y-range sensibly trimmed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches the figure content accurately; no legend needed (labels on axis); minor issue: no data.json not provided to verify numerical claims; caption mentions Gaussian kernel bandwidth=0.25 consistent with script.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is only 48 KB but uses Type 3 (bitmap) font rendering, not Type 42 (TrueType outline); script does not set matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is not selectable/editable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (48 KB), figure.png (65 KB), figure.svg (198 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the kernel type, bandwidth (0.25), color encoding, and provides specific qualitative findings (Clusters 1–2 lower/narrower; Clusters 3–6 higher median and heavier right tails); could add a concrete numerical value (e.g., median range) but is otherwise strong.

overall_strengths: Clear ridgeline layout with well-chosen overlap, clean spine removal, and left-axis cluster labels fulfills the brief exactly.
Caption is scientifically precise, naming kernel, bandwidth, color scheme, and directional finding across clusters.
overall_weaknesses: Typography is the critical failure: no pdf.fonttype=42 setting means the PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type 3 bitmap glyphs rather than editable Type 42 outlines, and SVG text is paths rather than selectable characters.
Palette uses red/blue sequential scheme that is not colorblind-safe for deuteranopes/protanopes; a perceptually-uniform sequential palette (e.g., viridis, cividis) would be more appropriate.
